reasoning: Initial state shows R1=10, R2=5, total_resistance=15. This looks like a series circuit (10+5=15). I need to verify this by changing one variable and seeing if the total resistance changes additively. I will increase R1 to 20 and observe the new total resistance.

The initial state showed R1=10, R2=5, total=15 (10+5=15). After increasing R1 to 20, R2 remained 5, and total became 25 (20+5=25). This strongly suggests a series circuit where total_resistance = R1 + R2. To confirm robustness, I will decrease R1 back to 10 and verify the total returns to 15.

I have confirmed that increasing R1 from 10 to 20 increased total_resistance from 15 to 25 (a difference of 10). Decreasing R1 back to 10 returned total_resistance to 15. This linear, additive relationship (delta R1 = delta total) strongly indicates a series circuit where total_resistance = R1 + R2. To further solidify this, I will change R2 to a different value and verify the additivity holds there.

I have observed that changing R1 by 10 units changes total_resistance by 10 units (additive). Changing R2 from 5 to 10 (delta +5) changed total_resistance from 15 to 20 (delta +5). This confirms the relationship total_resistance = R1 + R2. I will now decrease R2 back to 5 to return to the initial state and ensure reversibility before finishing.

I have performed multiple experiments confirming the additive relationship. Increasing R1 by 10 increased total_resistance by 10. Increasing R2 by 5 increased total_resistance by 5. Decreasing them back restored the original values. The evidence overwhelmingly supports total_resistance = R1 + R2. I will now finish and submit the final equation.
